title: Rotini CLI Spec Schema
description: Combined schema for a Rotini CLI spec file (.rotini.json)

$schema.description: URL identifying the rotini schema version. The version segment must match the installed rotini binary version.

short_description.description: Short description of the CLI shown in root help output

long_description.description: Long description of the CLI shown in root help output

examples.description: Example usage text shown in root help output

configs.description: Config files to load at startup

short_description.description: One-line description shown in parent help output

long_description.description: Detailed description shown in per-command help output

examples.description: Example usage text shown in per-command help output

usage.description: Custom usage string shown instead of auto-generated usage line

deprecated_message.description: Message shown when this command is used (deprecated only)

parameters.description: Parameters for this command (flags, arguments, env, config, stdin)

responses.description: Named response definitions for the command's Run handler

output.description: Default output format and stream for this command's responses

builtin.description: Opt-in built-in handler behavior. When set, generates a pre-written handler implementation instead of an empty stub.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in help output

deprecated_message.description: Message shown when this parameter is used (deprecated only)

sensitive.description: When true, value is treated as sensitive (not logged)

default.description: Default value when parameter is not provided

completions.description: Enable dynamic completion callback for this parameter

identifiers.description: CLI flag identifiers (e.g., '--force', '-f'). Only valid when in == 'flag'.

action.description: When true, the flag is an action flag (verb role): it triggers an operation and may define a typed response. When false (default), the flag is a modifier flag (adjective role). Only valid when in == 'flag'.

hidden.description: When true, the flag is hidden from help output. Only valid when in == 'flag'.

persistent.description: When true, the flag propagates to all subcommands. Only valid when in == 'flag'.

count.description: When true, counts flag repetitions as an integer value (e.g., -vvv = 3). Only valid when in == 'flag' and schema.type == 'int'.

placeholder.description: Custom placeholder shown in help usage lines. Only valid when in == 'flag'.

responses.description: Named response definitions for action flags. Only valid when in == 'flag' and action == true.

variadic.description: When true, collects all remaining positional values. Only valid when in == 'argument'.

stdin_dash.description: When true, '-' as the argument value reads from stdin. Only valid when in == 'argument'.

env.description: Environment variable name. Required when in == 'env'; also usable as fallback for flags and arguments.

file.description: Config file path. Only valid when in == 'config'.

key.description: Config file key path. Only valid when in == 'config'.

format.description: Input format (e.g., 'json', 'text'). Applicable to stdin and stdin_dash argument sources.

oneOf.description: Exactly one group of named parameters must be provided (mutually exclusive)

allOf.description: All named parameters in each group must be provided together

anyOf.description: At least one group of named parameters must satisfy the constraint

not.description: None of the named parameters in each group may be provided together

omit.description: When true, exclude this field from table output

format.description: Default output format for all responses

stream.description: Default output stream for all responses

param.description: Name of the parameter to check

equals.description: Condition is true when the parameter's value equals this string

present.description: Condition is true when the parameter was explicitly provided

when.description: All conditions must be true for this group to activate (allOf semantics). When absent, the group is unconditional.

min.description: Minimum number of positional arguments required

max.description: Maximum number of positional arguments allowed

exact.description: Exact number of positional arguments required

$ref.description: Reference to a component response definition (e.g. '#/definition/components/outputs/MyResponse'). Resolved at codegen time — the referenced output is inlined before code generation.

exit_code.description: Exit code for this response (0 for success, non-zero for error/warning)

format.description: Overrides command-level output format for this response only

stream.description: Overrides command-level output stream for this response only

$ref.description: Reference to a component schema definition (e.g. '#/definition/components/schemas/MySchema'). Resolved at codegen time — the referenced schema is inlined before code generation.

type.description: The type used to parse and store the value. Accepts both Go type names (bool, int, float64, []string, duration, map) and JSON Schema standard names (boolean, integer, number, array, object) — both are equivalent. Custom Go types are also accepted (e.g. 'url.URL') when the required package is listed in configuration.package.imports.

enum.description: Allowed values; parsed values are validated against this list

pattern.description: Regular expression the value must match (string type only)

display.description: Table rendering hints for this property

name.description: Logical name for the config file (e.g., 'app-config')

watch.description: When false, disables file watching for this config. Default true.

path.description: Base path for generated code. Framework is written to {path}/rotini/rotini.gen.go and handlers to {path}/cmd/rotini.gen.go.

imports.description: List of Go import paths to include in generated code. Each entry is a package path (e.g., 'net/url') or an aliased import (e.g., 'myalias net/url').
